gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The conventional sentinel that means \"read standard input\" across the toolchain CLIs."] pub const STDIN_SENTINEL : & str = "-" ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The error from [`read_source`]: the diagnostic has **already been emitted** to stderr (never-silent,"] # [doc = " G2); it carries no payload because there is nothing more to say — the caller's only job is to map it"] # [doc = " to *its* exit code (the bins use `66` / `EX_IOERR`). A distinct (non-`()`) type keeps the contract"] # [doc = " legible at the call site and satisfies the no-bare-`Result<_, ()>` lint without inventing data."] # [derive (Clone , Copy , Debug , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct ReadError ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Read one source: standard input when `path == \"-\"`, otherwise the file at `path`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never-silent (G2).** On any failure this prints a diagnostic to **stderr** — using `prefix` as"] # [doc = " the tool tag so the message is byte-identical to what the bin emitted by hand — and returns"] # [doc = " [`Err`]`(())`. The caller maps that to its own exit code (the bins use `66` / `EX_IOERR`); this"] # [doc = " keeps the *exit-code newtype* (`u8` vs `ExitCode`) at the call site, where it belongs, while the"] # [doc = " *I/O and the message* live here once."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `prefix` is the leading tool tag exactly as the bin wrote it, e.g. `\"mycfmt: io-error\"` for"] # [doc = " `mycfmt`, `\"io-error\"` (no tool name) for `myc-check`, `\"myc-lint: io-error\"` for `myc-lint`."] # [doc = " The emitted lines are then, verbatim:"] # [doc = " * stdin failure: `\"{prefix}: could not read stdin\"`"] # [doc = " * file failure:  `\"{prefix}: {path}: {e}\"`"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " matching the originals char-for-char."] pub fn read_source (prefix : & str , path : & str) -> Result < String , ReadError > { use std :: io :: Read ; if path == STDIN_SENTINEL { let mut s = String :: new () ; if std :: io :: stdin () . read_to_string (& mut s) . is_err () { eprintln ! ("{prefix}: could not read stdin") ; return Err (ReadError) ; } Ok (s) } else { std :: fs :: read_to_string (path) . map_err (| e | { eprintln ! ("{prefix}: {path}: {e}") ; ReadError }) } }
gaps.reason: function body contains a nested item declaration (e.g. a local `static`/`const`/`fn`) — this grammar fragment has no local-item production; only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a trailing expression map

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Collect every `.myc` file under `dir`, recursively, **sorted**."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Deterministic and total over a readable tree: hidden entries (names starting with `.`) and any"] # [doc = " `target/` directory are skipped, exactly as the prior `myc-lint` `walk`/`walk_into` and"] # [doc = " `myc-sec::collect_myc` did. A directory that cannot be read is an explicit [`Err`] carrying the"] # [doc = " `\"{path}: {e}\"` message the originals produced — **never** a silently-empty walk (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The returned paths are *relative to / rooted at* `dir` in the same way `read_dir` yields them"] # [doc = " (the caller does any `strip_prefix` display work, unchanged)."] pub fn walk_myc (dir : & Path) -> Result < Vec < PathBuf > , String > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; walk_into (dir , & mut out) ? ; out . sort () ; Ok (out) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: fn walk_into (dir : & Path , out : & mut Vec < PathBuf >) -> Result < () , String > { let entries = std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ())) ? ; let mut paths : Vec < PathBuf > = entries . filter_map (| e | e . ok () . map (| e | e . path ())) . collect () ; paths . sort () ; for path in paths { let name = path . file_name () . and_then (| s | s . to_str ()) . unwrap_or_default () ; if name . starts_with ('.') || name == "target" { continue ; } if path . is_dir () { walk_into (& path , out) ? ; } else if path . extension () . is_some_and (| x | x == "myc") { out . push (path) ; } } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: unsupported expression form `std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ())) ?`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A transparent cursor over `std::env::args().skip(1)` that names the one repeated arg-parsing idiom:"] # [doc = " \"this flag takes a value — consume the next token, or it's a usage error\"."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " It deliberately does **no** flag interpretation of its own; the bin still owns its `match` over"] # [doc = " option names (their option sets differ). This only removes the duplicated"] # [doc = " `match args.next() { Some(v) => …, None => return usage() }` boilerplate, behaviour-identically."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = " # use mycelium_cli_common::Args;"] # [doc = " // The bin's loop keeps full control of which flags exist and what they mean:"] # [doc = " let mut args = Args::from_env();"] # [doc = " while let Some(tok) = args.next() {"] # [doc = "     match tok.as_str() {"] # [doc = "         \"--config\" => match args.value() {"] # [doc = "             Some(v) => { /* use v */ let _ = v; }"] # [doc = "             None => { /* return usage() */ }"] # [doc = "         },"] # [doc = "         _ => { /* ... */ }"] # [doc = "     }"] # [doc = " }"] # [doc = " ```"] pub struct Args { inner : std :: vec :: IntoIter < String > , }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use std :: fs ; # [test] fn read_source_reads_a_file () { let dir = tmpdir ("read-file") ; let p = dir . join ("a.myc") ; fs :: write (& p , "nodule x\n") . unwrap () ; let got = read_source ("mycfmt: io-error" , p . to_str () . unwrap ()) ; assert_eq ! (got , Ok ("nodule x\n" . to_owned ())) ; let _ = fs :: remove_dir_all (& dir) ; } # [test] fn read_source_missing_file_is_err_not_silent () { let got = read_source ("mycfmt: io-error" , "definitely/not/here.myc") ; assert_eq ! (got , Err (ReadError)) ; } # [test] fn stdin_sentinel_is_exactly_dash () { assert_eq ! (STDIN_SENTINEL , "-") ; assert_eq ! (read_source ("io-error" , "-stdin-look-alike") , Err (ReadError)) ; } # [test] fn walk_myc_is_sorted_recursive_and_filters () { let dir = tmpdir ("walk") ; fs :: create_dir_all (dir . join ("sub")) . unwrap () ; fs :: create_dir_all (dir . join ("target")) . unwrap () ; fs :: create_dir_all (dir . join (".hidden")) . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join ("b.myc") , "") . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join ("a.myc") , "") . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join ("note.txt") , "") . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join ("sub/c.myc") , "") . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join ("target/skip.myc") , "") . unwrap () ; fs :: write (dir . join (".hidden/skip.myc") , "") . unwrap () ; let got = walk_myc (& dir) . unwrap () ; let rel : Vec < String > = got . iter () . map (| p | { p . strip_prefix (& dir) . unwrap () . to_string_lossy () . replace ('\\' , "/") }) . collect () ; assert_eq ! (rel , vec ! ["a.myc" , "b.myc" , "sub/c.myc"]) ; let _ = fs :: remove_dir_all (& dir) ; } # [test] fn walk_myc_unreadable_dir_is_err_not_silent () { let got = walk_myc (Path :: new ("definitely/not/a/dir")) ; assert ! (got . is_err ()) ; } # [test] fn walk_myc_empty_tree_is_ok_empty () { let dir = tmpdir ("walk-empty") ; let got = walk_myc (& dir) . unwrap () ; assert ! (got . is_empty ()) ; let _ = fs :: remove_dir_all (& dir) ; } # [test] fn args_value_consumes_next_token () { let mut a = Args :: from_args (["--config" , "proj.toml" , "file.myc"]) ; assert_eq ! (a . next () . as_deref () , Some ("--config")) ; assert_eq ! (a . value () . as_deref () , Some ("proj.toml")) ; assert_eq ! (a . next () . as_deref () , Some ("file.myc")) ; assert_eq ! (a . next () , None) ; } # [test] fn args_value_missing_is_none_for_usage () { let mut a = Args :: from_args (["--config"]) ; assert_eq ! (a . next () . as_deref () , Some ("--config")) ; assert_eq ! (a . value () , None) ; } fn tmpdir (tag : & str) -> PathBuf { let mut p = std :: env :: temp_dir () ; let nonce = std :: time :: SystemTime :: now () . duration_since (std :: time :: UNIX_EPOCH) . unwrap () . as_nanos () ; p . push (format ! ("mycelium-cli-common-{tag}-{nonce}")) ; fs :: create_dir_all (& p) . unwrap () ; p } }
